visa_types.name: Unorganized Tourism
visa_types.documents_required:
Please note, incomplete set of documents will not be accepted. It is impossible to add a document to set after submission. 
For citizens of Ukraine and permanent residents legally residing in Ukraine who wish to apply for a Unorganized tourism visa category following documents are required:
- Visa application form.
- Completed for each customer including children. It can be filled out on your computer or in a written form.
- Filled out in Russian or Bulgarian languages. Name and surname has to be spelled in English according to international passport, the form has to be signed personally by the customer
                                                                                in the field 48.
- For minors, the application has to be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian.
- One colour photograph.
- Size 35 x 45 mm.
- White background.
- Not older than 6 months.
- Passport or other travel document.
- International passport.
- Copy of the first page of international passport with personal information.
- Passport should be valid for at least three months after the date of visa expiration.
- Passport must be issued within the last 10 years and should contain at least two blank pages.
- Extended passports will not be accepted.
- Endorsed children 5 years or older must have the photo included in the parent’s passport.
- Medical  travel insurance.
- Coverage of 30.000 Euro.
- Valid for Bulgaria, Europe or the whole world and covering the duration of stay.
- Hotel or other reservation.
- If apartments are booked – invitation from the apartment owner, certified by a Bulgarian notary. It must contain the following information: passport details of each customer,
                                                                                the date of the intended stay, reservation number, apartment’s address and contact phone number.
- If the purpose of the trip is camping – it is required to specify in visa application form the address of the location in Bulgaria (the location of the tent camp), as well as
                                                                                to provide financial documents at the rate of € 100 per day of stay per person. It is also necessary to provide the itinerary and print the map with a mark of your location
                                                                                in Bulgaria.
Please note: The customers, who applies in VAC in Odessa has to provide prepaid reservation of the hotel of 30% of total amount
- Financial documents (at least one must be submitted).
- Work statement on the company letterhead indicating the salary of the customer for the last 6 months.
- Bank Statement that confirms solvency of 50 EUR per person per day but not less than 500 EUR for the whole journey (bank income statement, ATM check with copy of the card, currency
                                                                                exchange receipt etc.).
- Pensioner statement from the Pension Fund of Ukraine indicating the amount of pension.
- University certificate with accrual of scholarships for the last 6 months.
Please note: In case of providing hotel reservation with full payment (100%) financial documents are not obligatory for submission (except Odessa Visa Application
                                                                            Centre). Payment must be indicated in the hotel's letterhead or proof of money transfer should be provided.
- Proof of  intended transport (at least one must be presented). 
- Tickets reservation.
- Copy of the registration certificate of the vehicle and a copy of driver’s license for those who will travel by the own vehicle.
Necessary documents depending on the customer’s personal situation: 
- For private entrepreneurs:
- Certificate of registration of private entrepreneur.
- For  pensioners:
- Pension certificate or Statement from the Pension Fund of Ukraine.
- For  students: 
- Student certificate and Statement from the University.
- For  sponsorship:
- If the sponsor is a close relative of the customer: proof of kinship (a copy of the marriage/birth certificate) and sponsor’s financial documents. Close relatives are:
                                                                                        spouses, children.
- If the customer is not a close relative: a notarized declaration of sponsorship and sponsor’s financial documents.
- For foreign  citizens:
- Valid residence permit. It has to be valid for at least 3 months after return to Ukraine.
- For minors  (age under 18):
- Birth certificate.
- Copies of the first pages of parent’s internal passports.
- Notarized consent from both of the parents in case of a minor travelling alone or a notarized consent from the parent who is not travelling in case a minor travelling
                                                                                                with one of the parents. Visa Application Centres of Kyiv, Lviv, Donetsk, Kharkiv, Lutsk, Dnipropetrovsk and Ivano-Frankivsk accept notarized consent for travelling
                                                                                                abroad for children under 16 years. Visa Application Centre of Odessa accepts notarized consent for children under 18 years.
Please note: Except the international passport, the photo and the application form only photocopies of the documents are required. 
 At Odessa Visa Application Centre following documents should be submitted in originals:
- Bank Statement.
- Work statement.
- Currency exchange receipt.
- School/University certificate.

Processing time for Ukrainian nationals by usual process is 7-8 working days. In some cases consideration may be longer.
Processing time for citizens of those countries which did not sign the Visa Facilitation Agreement is 15 working days.
The day of submission is not included in the time of consideration.
We kindly advise all customers to plan their trips in advance and apply 2-3 weeks before the date of travel.
Please note that you may be required to come for a personal interview with the visa officer at the Embassy of Bulgaria in Kyiv prior to a decision being made on your application.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
For persons who wish to visit Bulgaria for unorganized tourism. This visa category is for those customers who prepare and submit documents for a visa on their own.
It allows the citizens of Ukraine and permanent residents legally residing in Ukraine to travel to Bulgaria and stay for a limited period of time of not more than 90 days in a 6 months period.
                                                                    
 Visa application can be submitted by:
- Customer himself.
- Close relatives: spouses, parents, adult children, grandmother / grandfather, adult grandchildren (appropriate documents need to be provided to confirm such relationships).
- By the third person (in this case notarized power of attorney needs be provided).
- For minors – only parents, legal guardians or anyone who has notarized letter of attorney for submission.
At Odessa Visa Application Centre submission of the documents is available for: 
- Customer himself.
- Parents/legal guardians (for minors).
According to the decision of the Council of Ministers of the Republic of Bulgaria №459 from 03 July 2014 it is possible to visit Bulgaria with a period of stay up to 90 days in 6 months period
                                                                    without Bulgarian national visa for:
- Holders of a short-term 2 or multiple entry Schengen visa and visa of Cyprus, Romania or Croatia.
- Holders of national visa (D) one of the countries of the Schengen Area or Cyprus, Romania and Croatia.
- Holders of residence permit in one of the countries of the Schengen Area or Cyprus, Romania and Croatia.
The customer will not be able to stay in Bulgaria for more days than specified in the visa. The number of days spent in Bulgaria is not deducted from the visa.
To read more about fees, the list of required documents, visa application processing time, as well as to download the necessary forms, please click on links above.

- For minors – only parents, legal guardians or anyone who has notarized letter of attorney for submission.
At Odessa Visa Application Centre submission of the documents is available for: 
- Customer himself.
- Parents/legal guardians (for minors).
According to the decision of the Council of Ministers of the Republic of Bulgaria №459 from 03 July 2014 it is possible to visit Bulgaria with a period of stay up to 90 days in 6 months period
                                                                    without Bulgarian national visa for:

To read more about fees, the list of required documents, visa application processing time, as well as to download the necessary forms, please click on links above. 

## Visa Fees

[Table:Unorganized Tourism]

Please note that all the fees are payable in Hryvnia (UAH) at the Cashier counter inside the Visa Application Centre according to the weekly exchange rate.
Credit card payments are available at the Visa Applications Centres in Kiev, Lviv, Kharkiv, Lutsk and Ivano-Frankivsk (only Visa card). 
Currency exchange is available at the Visa Applications Centres in Lviv, Kharkiv, Lutsk, Ivano-Frankivsk and Odessa.
Form filling service is available at the Visa Applications Centres in Kiev, Lviv, Kharkiv, Lutsk, Ivano-Frankivsk, Dnipropetrovsk and Odessa. 
The purchase of medical  insurance policy service is available at the Visa Centres in Kiev, Kharkiv and Odessa.

- Visa application form.

                                                                                in the field 48.

- Size 35 x 45 mm.
- White background.
- Not older than 6 months.
- Passport or other travel document.
- International passport.
- Copy of the first page of international passport with personal information.
- Passport should be valid for at least three months after the date of visa expiration.
- Passport must be issued within the last 10 years and should contain at least two blank pages.
- Extended passports will not be accepted.
- Endorsed children 5 years or older must have the photo included in the parent’s passport.
- Medical  travel insurance.
- Coverage of 30.000 Euro.
- Valid for Bulgaria, Europe or the whole world and covering the duration of stay.
- Hotel or other reservation.
- If apartments are booked – invitation from the apartment owner, certified by a Bulgarian notary. It must contain the following information: passport details of each customer,

                                                                                in Bulgaria.
Please note: The customers, who applies in VAC in Odessa has to provide prepaid reservation of the hotel of 30% of total amount

- Work statement on the company letterhead indicating the salary of the customer for the last 6 months.

                                                                                exchange receipt etc.).

- Proof of  intended transport (at least one must be presented). 
- Tickets reservation.
- Copy of the registration certificate of the vehicle and a copy of driver’s license for those who will travel by the own vehicle.
Necessary documents depending on the customer’s personal situation: 
- For private entrepreneurs:
- Certificate of registration of private entrepreneur.
- For  pensioners:
- Pension certificate or Statement from the Pension Fund of Ukraine.
- For  students:

                                                                                                abroad for children under 16 years. Visa Application Centre of Odessa accepts notarized consent for children under 18 years.

- Bank Statement.
- Work statement.
- Currency exchange receipt.
- School/University certificate.

## ATTENTION:
The Embassy/Consulate General of Bulgaria has the right to ask for any other additional documents necessary, may call the customer for an interview, and keeps the right for refusal without
                                                                    any explanation. In all cases the visa fee is non-refundable.
Please note that you may purchase medical insurance policy at the Visa Application Centre as well as order form filling by Visa Application Centre manager during submission
                                                                    process. 

## Photo Specifications
A photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:

- Must be an up-to-date photograph (preferably maximum 6 months old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 x 4,5 cm
- Close up to your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to head of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed

## Processing Time
Processing time for Ukrainian nationals by usual process is 7-8 working days. In some cases consideration may be longer.
Processing time for citizens of those countries which did not sign the Visa Facilitation Agreement is 15 working days.
The day of submission is not included in the time of consideration.
We kindly advise all customers to plan their trips in advance and apply 2-3 weeks before the date of travel.
Please note that you may be required to come for a personal interview with the visa officer at the Embassy of Bulgaria in Kyiv prior to a decision being made on your application.
Embassy also reserves the right to ask for additional supporting documents during the visa application process. customers are requested to read the information on this website carefully. The guidelines listed here are intended to help you to prepare your documents as accurately as possible. This will reduce the chances of your application being incomplete or delayed. 

## Application Form
Visa application form has to be completed for all the customers including minors electronically on your computer or in writing. The VAF has to be filled out in Russian or Bulgarian languages.
                                                                        Name and surname has to be spelled in English as in the foreign passport, the form needs to be signed personally by the customer in the field 48.
For minors, the application must be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian.
New Visa application form can be downloaded and printed from the link below. Forms are also available at the Visa Application Centre (free of charge).
Please note that the Visa application Centre is introducing the Form  Filling service for the convenience of the customers at the Centre.The service is purely optional and the customers may avail this service  at a nominal price of UAH85 (all taxes included). Please contact the VAC staff to know more about the Form Filling  service.

[Table:Unorganized Tourism]

To view the example of filling the application form for the visa category "Unorganized tourism" please use the link below. 
According to the Law of Ukraine About personal information, Personal Data Information Protection Agreement has been introduced and is required to be signed by each customer applying for
                                                                        a visa through the Visa Centers. For minors this agreement should be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian. We pay you attention that such agreement has to be signed personally
                                                                        by the customer even if submitting documents through a representative or accredited travel agency. 

[Table:Unorganized Tourism]

visa_types.overview:
Overview
For persons who wish to visit Bulgaria for organized tourism. This visa category is for those customers who prepare and submit documents for a visa through a travel agency. Only accredited
                                                                        travel agencies can apply documents to the Visa Application Centre. It allows the citizens of Ukraine and permanent residents legally residing in Ukraine to travel to Bulgaria and
                                                                        stay for a limited period of time of not more than 90 days in a 6 months period. 
For detailed information on how to obtain accreditation at the Embassy please contact the Embassy/General Consulate of Bulgaria directly. 
 Submitting the documents to the Visa Application Centre a travel agent has to submit a power of attorney issued on the letterhead of the agency with the following information: passport
                                                                        data of the travel agent, passport data of tourists as well as the date of departure of tourists. The same power of attorney should be presented during passport collection. 
For a one year visa it is required to submit a copy of the previous 6 months Bulgarian visa.
According to the decision of the Council of Ministers of the Republic of Bulgaria №459 from 03 July 2014 it is possible to visit Bulgaria with a period of stay up to 90 days in 6 months
                                                                        period without Bulgarian national visa for:
- Holders of a short-term 2 or multiple entry Schengen visa and visa of Cyprus, Romania or Croatia.
- Holders of national visa (D) one of the countries of the Schengen Area or Cyprus, Romania and Croatia.
- Holders of residence permit in one of the countries of the Schengen Area or Cyprus, Romania and Croatia.
The customer will not be able to stay in Bulgaria for more days than specified in the visa. The number of days spent in Bulgaria is not deducted from the visa.
Representatives of travel agencies must provide Personal Data Information Protection  Agreement for each customer, signed by the customer personally.
To read more about fees, the list of required documents, visa application processing time, as well as to download the necessary forms, please click on links above.

- Pension certificate or Statement from the Pension Fund of Ukraine.
- For  students: 
- Student certificate and Statement from the University.

- If the sponsor is a close relative of the customer: proof of kinship (a copy of the marriage/birth certificate) and sponsor’s financial documents.
Close        relatives are:
spouses, children.
- If the customer is not a close relative: a notarized declaration of sponsorship and sponsor’s financial documents.
- If it is specified in the invitation that the hosting company takes care of all financial costs, the customer can submit his financial documents upon his discretion.
- For foreign  citizens:
-  Valid residence permit. It has to be valid for at least 3 months after return to Ukraine.

 At Odessa Visa Application Centre following documents should be submitted in originals:

- Work statement.
- Currency exchange receipt.

                                                                        process. 

## Photo Specifications
A photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:

- Must be an up-to-date photograph (preferably maximum 6 months old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 x 4,5 cm
- Close up to your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to head of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed

## Processing Time
Processing time for Ukrainian nationals by usual process is 7-8 working days. In some cases consideration may be longer.
Processing time for citizens of those countries which did not sign the Visa Facilitation Agreement is 15 working days.
The day of submission is not included in the time of consideration.
We kindly advise all customers to plan their trips in advance and apply 2-3 weeks before the date of travel.
Please note that you may be required to come for a personal interview with the visa officer at the Embassy of Bulgaria in Kyiv prior to a decision being made on your application.
Embassy also reserves the right to ask for additional supporting documents during the visa application process. customers are requested to read the information on this website carefully. The guidelines listed here are intended to help you to prepare your documents as accurately as possible. This will reduce the chances of your application being incomplete or delayed.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
For persons who have their own property in Bulgaria. 
It allows the citizens of Ukraine and permanent residents legally residing in Ukraine to travel to Bulgaria and stay for a limited period of time of not more than 90 days in a 6 months
                                                                        period. 
Visa can be issued with validity up to 3 years without additional documents. For minor children the visa can be issued only to their age of majority. If the person aged from 18 to 21
                                                                        year studies on full-time type of education, the visa can be issued till 21 years only. If the property has been bought after the age of majority of the child, and his name is not
                                                                        mentioned in the notarized act, then the child can't apply for this category of visa. 
 Visa application can be submitted by:
- Customer himself.
- Close relatives: spouses, parents, adult children, grandmother / grandfather, adult grandchildren (appropriate documents need to be provided to confirm such relationships).
- By the third person (in this case notarized power of attorney needs be provided).
- For minors – only parents, legal guardians or anyone who has notarized letter of attorney for submission.
At Odessa Visa Application Centre submission of the documents is available for:
- Customer himself.
- Parents/legal guardians (for minors).
According to the decision of the Council of Ministers of the Republic of Bulgaria №459 from 03 July 2014 it is possible to visit Bulgaria with a period of stay up to 90 days in 6 months
                                                                        period without Bulgarian national visa for:
- Holders of a short-term 2 or multiple entry Schengen visa and visa of Cyprus, Romania or Croatia.
- Holders of national visa (D) one of the countries of the Schengen Area or Cyprus, Romania and Croatia.
- Holders of residence permit in one of the countries of the Schengen Area or Cyprus, Romania and Croatia.
The customer will not be able to stay in Bulgaria for more days than specified in the visa. The number of days spent in Bulgaria is not deducted from the visa.
To read more about fees, the list of required documents, visa application processing time, as well as to download the necessary forms, please click on links above.
